title: oci: report empty exec path as ENOENT
body:
unify the error codes returned by runc and crun.

Fix the tests to work with both runtimes, as well as the https://github.com/containers/crun/pull/1672 changes in progress for crun.

Follow-up for https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25340

comments.body:
a follow-up for https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25340

I've tried the same tests with runc (and some of them were failing).  I think the runc error message is better when the exec path is empty, so I've changed crun to follow that

@baude @Luap99 PTAL, it is a follow-up for https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25340

I think the error returned by runc is better, so I've changed the crun PR to do the same.  IMO, an empty path must return ENOENT, not EPERM

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe*, *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99,giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

/lgtm

I hate how fragile this is, but unless the OCI decides to standardize things like this and make it the concern of the runtime, seems like the best we can do.

@giuseppe: #25350 failed to apply on top of branch "v5.4-rhel":
